summary: Large WIP matching-progress PR that implemented many previously stubbed functions across fighters, Kirby copy abilities, stages, items, menus, game-mode clearing, THP playback, and fighter color animation. The PR was closed unmerged and split into themed PRs: #2341 stages, #2342 items, #2343 Kirby, and #2344 misc. The dump reports 32 matched functions, 11 failed attempts, and 1 pending function; fn_80200460 was explicitly excluded from the split because it was only a 98.2% match.
what_changed: Replaced many /// #function stubs with matched C implementations. The PR body reports 32 matched functions, including Kirby neutral-special functions, stage helpers, item callbacks, menu logic, THP playback helpers, and game-mode clearing.

Implemented ftCo_Attack100.c fn_800DA1D8 as a grab-hold transition: zeroes ground velocity, changes to ftCo_MS_CatchWait, installs accessory and damage callbacks, stores a capturedamage joint, spawns effect 0x41D, updates common fighter state, and syncs the victim via fn_800DB6C8.

Implemented Crazy Hand ftCh_Damage2_Anim, using ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining, mpFloorGetRight, current X position, Crazy Hand dat attrs, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftAnim_8006EBA4, and ft_PlaySFX to either continue damage recoil or transition to another Crazy Hand action.

Implemented Kirby hat material-animation helper ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C, walking HSD_MatAnimJoint data selected by costume id and applying matanims to Kirby hat DObjs while skipping parts reported by ftParts_8007506C.

Implemented Kirby swallow/drink animation callbacks ftKb_SpecialNDrink0_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNDrink1_Anim, and ftKb_SpecialAirNDrink_Anim, including item/victim handling, sound playback, target cleanup, copy-ability setup, and fall/wait transitions when animation frames end.

Implemented Kirby copied Jigglypuff Rollout air-start callback ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim, transitioning to ftKb_MS_PrSpecialAirNFull, setting damage/death/deal-damage callbacks, stopping animation, seeding tiny horizontal velocity, and rotating the part to M_PI_2.

Implemented Kirby copied Sheik Needle Storm loop IASA callbacks for ground and air. They release into end states when B is no longer held, set accessory4_cb to fn_80106DB0, or cancel when L/R is pressed.

Implemented Kirby copied Mewtwo neutral-special ground start ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568 via an inline helper that changes motion state, clears cmd_vars, clears common state, resets velocity and callbacks, initializes motion variables, conditionally loads specialn_mt_frames_to_transition, and calls ftAnim_8006EBA4.

Implemented Ice Climbers ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow_0_Anim, handling animation-end fall-special setup, Nana coordination through Player_GetEntityAtIndex and ftNn_Init_8012309C, and ongoing Popo motion bookkeeping.

Implemented ftCo_800C0134 in ftcolanim.c to reset fighter overlay state, call a per-kind callback from ftData_UnkMotionStates4, and reapply hammer glow/color animation when ftCo_800C53E4 indicates it is needed.

Adjusted game-mode header prototypes to more specific types and implemented gmregclear fn_8017F1B8, iterating 256 bonus/award kinds and clearing entries depending on decision type and save/registry checks before calling fn_8016FFD4.
Implemented grAnime_801C83D0 by retrieving an HSD_AObj and testing flag 0x40000000 through HSD_AObjGetFlags.

Implemented stage-related functions in grcorneria.c, grkongo.c, grrcruise.c, grhomerun.c, groldpupupu.c, and supporting gr/types.h changes; the PR body describes Corneria joint/world position math, Kongo bridge plank/barrel logic, Rainbow Cruise platform/collision handling, Home Run distance meter initialization, and Dream Land wind selection.

Implemented item-related functions in itkamex.c, itkirbycutterbeam.c, itlugia.c, itoldkuri.c, and itpikachuthunder.c; the PR body describes Blastoise motion timing, Kirby Cutter beam spawning, Lugia projectile spawning, Oldkuri wall/slope physics, and Pikachu Thunder collision behavior.

Implemented miscellaneous matched functions in lbmthp.c, mnname.c, pltrick.c, and toy.c, covering THP playback/header handling, name-entry selection highlight updates, player attack statistics tracking, and trophy-view camera reset behavior.
smart_moves: The author tracked matching status per function in the PR body, separating matched, failed, and pending work. That makes the PR useful as a matching ledger even though it was later split.

Several implementations used exact callback and state-field restoration rather than only control-flow reconstruction, such as ftCo_Attack100 fn_800DA1D8 setting accessory1_cb, take_dmg_cb, capturedamage joint, effects, and victim synchronization.

Kirby copy-ability work reused existing fighter helpers and state conventions: Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftKirbyDmgInline, ftCommon_8007D7FC, ftAnim_8006EBA4, ftAnim_SetAnimRate, ftPartSetRotY, and copy-specific callbacks.
Header prototypes were tightened from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures where evidence was available, improving future callsite typing and reducing unknown propagation.

The Kirby Mewtwo start implementation factored the matching-sensitive body into a static inline helper and left the public function as a thin wrapper with PAD_STACK, a common decomp tactic when stack shape or inlining affects the match.
Matching pragmas were used in ftcolanim.c around ftCo_800C0134, with auto_inline and global_optimizer disabled, showing compiler-control pragmas were necessary for at least one function.
The PR was split by domain after feedback or self-review into stages, items, Kirby, and misc, reducing review surface and keeping unrelated matched functions out of a single mega-PR.
decomp_lessons: Large cross-system matching batches are hard to review; split into themed PRs before final submission when the changes cover fighters, stages, items, libraries, and menus at once.
When replacing stubs, update headers in the same PR to concrete return and parameter types, especially for callback-heavy systems where wrong prototypes can hide ABI or stack-shape issues.

For fighter callbacks, a match often depends on preserving the exact state-machine side effects: motion state flags, command variables, callbacks, effect spawns, victim links, animation frame/rate fields, and velocity fields.
Kirby copy-ability code frequently uses copied-character-specific state packed into existing Kirby motion fields; avoid renaming or restructuring those fields without strong evidence.
Stage code often needs small local struct overlays over static data blobs, as seen in grCorneria data declarations and gr/types.h adjustments. Keep such structs minimal and evidence-based.
Use PAD_STACK and local-scope redeclarations when necessary for matching, but keep them localized and document them through the matched function rather than broad refactors.
A near-match such as fn_80200460 at 98.2% should not be bundled as if complete; excluding it from split PRs preserved the distinction between fully matched and still-problematic code.
Library and animation helpers may require adding specific baselib includes, such as baselib/aobj.h for HSD_AObjGetFlags, rather than relying on indirect declarations.
naming_conventions: Existing address-style placeholder names were preserved where semantic names were not yet established, such as fn_800DA1D8, fn_8017F1B8, fn_80200460, and it_802D1E8C.

Character/action names follow the project’s fighter prefix convention: ftKb for Kirby, ftCh for Crazy Hand, ftPp for Popo, ftNn for Nana, ftCo for common fighter code, with suffixes like _Anim and _IASA for callback type.

Kirby copied-special names encode copied character abbreviations and action states, such as ftKb_SkSpecialNLoop_IASA for Sheik copy, ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim for Jigglypuff/Purin copy, and ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568 for Mewtwo copy.
Stage functions use gr plus stage names or abbreviations: grCorneria, grKongo, grRCruise, grHomeRun, grOldPupupu, and grAnime.
Item files retain item/Pokémon names from the project convention, including itkamex, itlugia, itoldkuri, itpikachuthunder, and itkirbycutterbeam.
When types were not fully understood, local names such as grCn_Entry and grCn_Data were introduced narrowly around the evidence in grcorneria.c rather than generalized beyond the matched function.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in multiple matched functions, including fn_800DA1D8, ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C, ftKb_SpecialAirNDrink_Anim, ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568, and ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow_0_Anim.
Used comma-expression GET_FIGHTER patterns such as Fighter* fp = (0, GET_FIGHTER(gobj)); in Kirby drink animation callbacks, likely to preserve generated code shape.
Used nested scopes and repeated GET_FIGHTER/gobj->user_data reloads in callback implementations to match register allocation and lifetimes.
Used static const motion flag composition in ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim: (1 << 1) | (1 << 4) | (1 << 18).
Used a static inline helper for ftKb_SpecialNMt_ChangeAction so the exported ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568 could remain a wrapper with the desired stack behavior.
Used #pragma auto_inline off and #pragma global_optimizer off around ftCo_800C0134, indicating optimizer behavior mattered for the match.
Preserved float literal forms and exact state parameters in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls, including 0.0F/1.0F and integer zero variants where shown.
Matched grAnime_801C83D0 with a simple flag test on HSD_AObjGetFlags(aobj) & 0x40000000, avoiding unnecessary boolean simplification beyond the observed return structure.

Changed prototypes from unknown forms to concrete signatures only where the implementation established them, for example void fn_800DA1D8(Fighter_GObj*), void fn_8017F1B8(void), int fn_8016F160(int, int), int fn_8016F1F0(int idx), and void fn_8016FFD4(struct lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t*, int, int).

review_feedback.text: Split into themed PRs: #2341 (stages), #2342 (items), #2343 (Kirby), #2344 (misc). Excluded fn_80200460 (98.2% match).
No line review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #2341 for the final reviewed stage-only subset split from this WIP.
Inspect PR #2342 for the final reviewed item-only subset split from this WIP.
Inspect PR #2343 for the final reviewed Kirby copy-ability subset split from this WIP.
Inspect PR #2344 for the final reviewed miscellaneous subset split from this WIP.
Search for later work on fn_80200460 to see how the 98.2% Rainbow Cruise collision callback was eventually resolved.
Search for grShrineRoute_8020AC44 to see whether the pending function from this PR was later matched.
Search failed functions listed in the PR body, including grPura_80212FC0, grOnett_801E5194, pl_80038628, grCastle_801CE19C, InitNameEntryUIState, gm_801BAC9C, ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0, and mn_80230198.
